volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 1583a - Section 2.1744)

sections.label: Section 1583a
sections.translation: You may be sure that he has left this life .

sections.label: Section 1585
sections.original: πῶς ; ἆρα θείᾳ κἀπόνῳ τάλας τύχῃ ;
sections.translation: How ? By a fate divine and painless , the poor man ?

sections.label: Section 1586
sections.translation: In that you touch upon what is indeed worthy of wonder . How he departed from here , you yourself must know since you were here : with no one of his friends as guide , but rather with himself leading the way for us all .

sections.label: Section 1590
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δʼ ἀφῖκτο τὸν καταρράκτην ὀδὸν
sections.translation: When he had come to the Descending Way , which is bound by steps of bronze to earth ’ s deep roots , he paused at one of the many branching paths near the basin in the rock , where the faithful covenant of Theseus and Peirithous has its memorial .

sections.label: Section 1595
sections.original: ἀφʼ οὗ μέσος στὰς τοῦ τε Θορικίου πέτρου
sections.translation:
He stood midway between that basin and the Leaping stone , and between the hollow pear-tree and the marble tomb ; then he sat down and loosened his filthy clothing .
And then he called his daughters , and asked them to bring water from some flowing source , so that he might wash and make a drink-offering .

sections.label: Section 1600
sections.original: τὼ δʼ εὐχλόου Δήμητρος εἰς προσόψιον
sections.translation:
They went to the hill which was in view , the hill of Demeter who guards the tender plants , and in a short time brought what their father had commanded . Then they washed him and dressed him , as is the custom .
But when all his desire was fulfilled ,

sections.label: Section 1605
sections.original: κοὐκ ἦν ἔτʼ οὐδὲν ἀργὸν ὧν ἐφίετο ,
sections.translation: and nothing that he required was still undone , then Zeus of the Underworld sent forth his thunder , and the maidens shuddered as they heard . They fell weeping at their father ’ s knees , and did not cease from beating their breast , and from wailing loud .

sections.label: Section 1610
sections.original: ὁ δʼ ὡς ἀκούει φθόγγον ἐξαίφνης πικρόν ,
sections.translation: When he heard their sudden bitter cry , he put his arms around them and said : My children , on this day your father no longer exists . Now I have perished utterly , and no longer will you bear the burden of tending me ,

sections.label: Section 1615
sections.original: σκληρὰν μέν , οἶδα , παῖδες · ἀλλʼ ἓν γὰρ μόνον
sections.translation: which was no light one , I well know , my children . Yet just one word turns all those toils to nothing : you have been treated as friends by no one more than by this man ; and now you will have me with you no longer , through all your days to come .

sections.label: Section 1620
sections.translation: In this way , clinging close to one another , the father and his daughters sobbed and wept . But when they came to the end of their crying , and the sound of wailing went forth no more , there was a silence ; suddenly a voice called aloud to him , so that everyone

sections.label: Section 1625
sections.translation:
felt hair rising from the sudden terror . The god called him again and again : Oedipus , Oedipus , why do you delay our going ? Too long you have been lingering .
And when he perceived that he was called by the god ,

sections.label: Section 1630
sections.original: αὐδᾷ μολεῖν οἱ γῆς ἄνακτα Θησέα .
sections.translation: he asked that lord Theseus should come to him ; and when he did , he said : Friend , give me the sworn pledge of your right hand for my children ; and you , my daughters , for him . Promise never to betray them by your own free will , but always to accomplish whatever you think for their benefit .

sections.label: Section 1635
sections.original: μέλλῃς φρονῶν εὖ ξυμφέροντʼ αὐτοῖς ἀεί .
sections.translation:
And he , as a man of noble spirit , without lamentation swore to keep that promise to the stranger .
When Theseus had done this , straightway Oedipus felt for his children with blind hands , and said :

sections.label: Section 1640
sections.original: ὦ παῖδε , τλάσας χρὴ τὸ γενναῖον φρενὶ
sections.translation: Children , you must bear up nobly in your hearts and depart from this place ; do not consider it just to look upon what is not right , or to hear such speech as you may not hear . Go in haste ; let only Theseus be entitled to remain to learn of those things which will be done .

sections.label: Section 1645
sections.translation: So he spoke , and everyone of us listened ; with streaming tears and mourning we followed the maidens away . But when we had gone off , very soon we looked back and saw that Oedipus was nowhere any more and our lord was alone ,

sections.label: Section 1650
sections.translation: holding his hand in front of his face to screen his eyes , as if he had seen some terrifying sight , one that no one could endure to behold . And then after a short time ,

sections.label: Section 1655
sections.original: καὶ τὸν θεῶν Ὄλυμπον ἐν ταὐτῷ λόγῳ .
sections.translation:
we saw him adore together the earth and Olympus of the gods in the same prayer .
But by what fate Oedipus perished , no man can tell , except Theseus alone . It was no fiery thunderbolt of the god that removed him ,

sections.label: Section 1660
sections.original: θύελλα κινηθεῖσα τῷ τότʼ ἐν χρόνῳ ,
sections.translation: nor any rising of whirlwind from the sea ; it was either an escort from the gods , or else the dark world of the dead kindly split open to receive him . The man passed away without lamentation or sickness or suffering , and beyond all mortal men he was wondrous .

sections.label: Section 1665
sections.original: θαυμαστός . εἰ δὲ μὴ δοκῶ φρονῶν λέγειν ,
sections.translation: And if in anyone ’ s eyes I seem to speak senselessly , I would not try to win his belief when he counts me senseless .

sections.label: Section 1667
sections.original: ποῦ δʼ αἵ τε παῖδες χοἰ προπέμψαντες φίλων ;
sections.translation: Where are his daughters and the escort of their friends ?

sections.label: Section 1668
sections.original: αἵδʼ οὐχ ἑκάς · γόων γὰρ οὐκ ἀσήμονες
sections.translation: Not far away ; the sounds of mourning show plainly that they are approaching .

sections.label: Section 1.1670
sections.original: αἰαῖ , φεῦ , ἔστιν ἔστι νῷν δὴ
sections.translation: Ah , me , ah , me ! Now , indeed , is it for us to bewail in full the curse on our blood — ill-fated sisters as we are — deriving from our father ! In former time we bore the long toil without pause ,

sections.label: Section 1.1675
sections.translation: and now at the last we bring to tell a sight and experience that baffle reason .

sections.label: Section 1.1677
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 1.1677a
sections.translation: It is possible to conjecture , friends .

sections.label: Section 1.1678
sections.translation: He is gone ?

sections.label: Section 1.1678a
sections.original: ὡς μάλιστʼ ἂν ἐν πόθῳ λάβοις .
sections.translation: Precisely in the way you could most wish for : indeed , in a way in which neither Ares took him , nor the sea ,

sections.label: Section 1.1680
sections.translation: but instead he was snatched away to the fields which no one may see , by some swift , strange doom . Wretched me ! For us a night like death has descended on our eyes ;

sections.label: Section 1.1685
sections.translation: how shall we find our hard livelihood , roaming to some far land , or on the waves of the sea ?

sections.label: Section 1.1690
sections.original: τάλαιναν , ὡς ἔμοιγʼ ὁ μέλλων βίος οὐ βιωτός .
sections.translation: Wretched me ! I cannot live the life that must be mine .

sections.label: Section 1.1692
sections.translation: Best of daughters , you both must bear the will of the gods . Do not be inflamed with too much grief ;

sections.label: Section 1.1695
sections.original: μηδὲν ἄγαν φλέγεσθον · οὔ τοι κατάμεμπτʼ ἔβητον .
sections.translation: what you have encountered is not to be blamed .

sections.label: Section 1.1700
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ ὃ μηδαμὰ δὴ φίλον ἦν φίλον ,
sections.translation: Father , Dear , clothed in the darkness of the underworld forever ! Never in your absence will you not be dear to me and to my sister here .

sections.label: Section 1.1704
sections.original: οὐδέ γʼ ἔνερθʼ ἀφίλητος ἐμοί ποτε
sections.translation: He fared —

sections.label: Section 1.1705
sections.translation: In what way ?

sections.label: Section 1.1710
sections.translation: nor do I know how in my wretchedness I must still my grief for you that is so immense . Alas ! You wanted to die in a foreign land , but you died without me near .

sections.label: Section 1.1714
sections.translation: Wretched me ! What fate

sections.label: Section 1.1715
sections.original: ὦ τάλαινα , τίς ἄρα με πότμος αὖθις ὧδʼ
sections.translation: awaits you and me , dear , orphaned as we are of our father ?

sections.label: Section 1.1720
sections.translation: Cease from your grief , dear girls , since his end is blessed . No one is beyond the reach of evil .

sections.label: Section 2.1723
sections.translation: Dear , let us hasten back .

sections.label: Section 2.1724
sections.translation: To do what deed ?

sections.label: Section 2.1725
sections.translation: A longing fills my soul —

sections.label: Section 2.1726
sections.translation: For what ?

sections.label: Section 2.1727
sections.original: θέμις δὲ πῶς τάδʼ ἐστί ; μῶν
sections.translation: To see the netherworld home .

sections.label: Section 2.1728
sections.translation: Of whom ?

sections.label: Section 2.1730
sections.translation: Surely you understand ?

sections.label: Section 2.1731
sections.translation: And surely you know this , too —

sections.label: Section 2.1731a
sections.translation: What more would you tell me ?

sections.label: Section 2.1732
sections.translation: That he perished without a tomb , apart from everyone .

sections.label: Section 2.1733
sections.translation: Lead me there , and then kill me , too .

sections.label: Section 2.1734
sections.translation: Unhappy me ! Abandoned and helpless ,

sections.label: Section 2.1735
sections.translation: where am I now to live my wretched life ?

sections.label: Section 2.1737
sections.translation: Dear girls , do not be afraid .

sections.label: Section 2.1738
sections.translation: But where shall I flee ?

sections.label: Section 2.1740
sections.original: τὰ σφῷν τὸ μὴ πίτνειν κακῶς .
sections.translation: — That no harm befall you .

sections.label: Section 2.1741
sections.translation: I feel —

sections.label: Section 2.1741a
sections.translation: What do you think ?

sections.label: Section 2.1742
sections.translation: How we are to go home , I cannot tell .

sections.label: Section 2.1743a
sections.translation: Do not seek to go .

sections.label: Section 2.1744
sections.translation: Trouble surrounds us .
